title: sorbus carpatica
intro: Sorbus carpatica, also known as the Carpathian whitebeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree species native to the Carpathian Mountains in Eastern Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues and skin problems, thanks to its high content of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Sorbus carpatica include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin, as well as aid in the healing of wounds and ulcers. Sorbus carpatica can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and enjoy its various health benefits.
